questions.question: Describe the living situation of Sue and Johnsy.
questions.answer: Sue and Johnsy were two young artists who shared a small flat on the third floor of an old building. They lived together as friends and supported each other while pursuing their artistic careers.

questions.question: Explain Johnsy’s illness and condition at the beginning of the story.
questions.answer: At the beginning of the story, Johnsy becomes seriously ill with pneumonia. She loses her will to live and spends her time lying in bed, staring out of the window and counting the falling ivy leaves.

questions.question: Why was Sue worried about Johnsy?
questions.answer: Sue was worried because Johnsy had lost hope and believed she would die when the last leaf fell from the ivy creeper. Her negative thinking made her illness worse.

questions.question: What did the doctor say about Johnsy’s recovery?
questions.answer: The doctor explained that medicine alone could not cure Johnsy. She needed to have the will to live, otherwise her chances of recovery were very small.

questions.question: Describe the ivy creeper outside the window.
questions.answer: An old ivy creeper grew on the wall outside the window. During autumn, its leaves fell one by one in the strong wind and rain.

questions.question: Why was Johnsy counting the leaves?
questions.answer: Johnsy believed that her life was connected to the ivy leaves. She thought she would die when the last leaf fell from the creeper.

questions.question: Who was Behrman and where did he live?
questions.answer: Behrman was an old painter who lived on the ground floor of the same building as Sue and Johnsy.

questions.question: What was Behrman’s dream?
questions.answer: Behrman dreamed of painting a masterpiece, although he had not been able to achieve it throughout his life.

questions.question: How did Sue try to encourage Johnsy?
questions.answer: Sue tried to encourage Johnsy by talking to her, painting in her room, and trying to distract her from her negative thoughts.

questions.question: Describe Behrman’s reaction to Johnsy’s belief.
questions.answer: Behrman was angry and called Johnsy foolish for believing that she would die when the last leaf fell.

questions.question: Describe the stormy night in the story.
questions.answer: On the stormy night, heavy rain and strong winds blew outside. Despite the harsh weather, the last leaf appeared to remain on the creeper.

questions.question: What surprised Johnsy the next morning?
questions.answer: Johnsy was surprised to see that the last leaf was still on the ivy creeper despite the storm.

questions.question: How did Johnsy’s attitude change after seeing the leaf?
questions.answer: Johnsy began to regain hope and realized that she should not wish for death.

questions.question: What did Johnsy do after she started feeling better?
questions.answer: Johnsy combed her hair, smiled, and began to take interest in life again.

questions.question: What did the doctor say about Johnsy’s recovery?
questions.answer: The doctor said that Johnsy would soon recover because she had regained her will to live.

questions.question: What happened to Behrman after the storm?
questions.answer: Behrman caught pneumonia after going out in the storm and died two days later.

questions.question: What objects were found near Behrman’s bed?
questions.answer: A ladder, a lantern, and painting materials were found near Behrman’s bed.

questions.question: What truth did Sue reveal to Johnsy?
questions.answer: Sue revealed that the last leaf was actually painted by Behrman during the storm.

questions.question: Why is the painted leaf called Behrman’s masterpiece?
questions.answer: It is called his masterpiece because it saved Johnsy’s life and gave her hope to live.

questions.question: Explain the theme of hope in the story.
questions.answer: The story shows that hope can help people overcome illness and despair.

questions.question: How does the story show the importance of friendship?
questions.answer: Sue’s constant care and encouragement show the importance of true friendship.

questions.question: What lesson does Johnsy learn from the last leaf?
questions.answer: Johnsy learns that life is valuable and that she should not lose hope.

questions.question: Why is Behrman remembered as a hero?
questions.answer: Behrman is remembered as a hero because he sacrificed his life to save Johnsy.

questions.question: Explain the importance of the last leaf in the story.
questions.answer: The last leaf becomes a symbol of hope and inspires Johnsy to fight her illness.

questions.question: Summarise the central message of the story.
questions.answer: The central message of the story is that hope, friendship, and selfless sacrifice can change lives.
